response: {"calibration_score":-0.15,"overconfident_count":3,"underconfident_count":0,"well_calibrated_count":3,"biases_detected":["anchoring: cargo PATH assumption repeated across units 1, 5, 7, 10 despite consistent prior failure — agent re-anchors to bare `cargo build` each session start","optimism bias: environment assumed to be configured (PATH, binary location, daemon socket) before verifying, leading to predictable first-try failures","sunk cost: unit-5 shell script testing pursued through 5 failing invocations (json.tool parse, INSIGHTS_FILE env var, sourcing) rather than abandoning the approach after second failure","availability bias: daemon management in unit-10 escalated to LaunchAgent creation and manual kill/restart before checking the simpler `i-dream start` subcommand path"],"recommendations":["establish a session-start environment probe — resolve cargo binary path once, export as variable, reuse throughout; this single fix would eliminate ~30% of observed tool failures","set a retry budget of 2 for any shell command pattern; on third failure, switch strategy rather than varying invocation syntax","for exploration units (4, 7) apply a read-budget discipline: form a hypothesis about file structure before opening files, stop reading once hypothesis is confirmed or refuted","daemon lifecycle operations should follow verify-then-act: check pid file and process table before issuing start/stop commands rather than issuing commands and reading state afterward","after any tool failure, log the failure class (PATH, missing file, permission, parse error) and match against a known-failure registry before retrying — unit-10 would have caught the PATH class from unit-1 in the same session"]}
